revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:建御名方彦神別命]]
{{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|parents={{ill|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|1=Takeminakata}}; {{ill|Yasakatome|qid=Q9150392|1=Yasakatome}}}}

'''Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto''' (彦神別命) is a [[kami (Shinto)]] deity found in [[:en:folk traditions|folk traditions]] and is associated with the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2370082}}.  
[[File:Yokoyamajyo.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Torii with the inscription "Takeminakata & Minomikotohikokamiwake Shrine," written by Prince Arisugawa Takahito.]]

== Overview ==
Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto is enshrined at the {{ill|Takeminakata & Minomikotohikokamiwake Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata & Minomikotohikokamiwake Shrine|qq=}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) located in {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11548296}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|qid=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}. This kami is regarded as the eldest son of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}.<ref>Norikawa Kazuhiko, "Supplement to Suwa Clan Genealogy," *Suwa Clan Genealogy, Volume 1*, Kōtarō Iida, Taisho 10, p. 14.</ref>  

The deity first appears in literature in the fifth year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|qid=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}} (691 CE), when it is recorded that "Minouchi-no-Kami" was enshrined in Shinano Province at the emperor's command.<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Jitō 5th year, 8th month, 23rd day entry.</ref> While this "Minouchi-no-Kami" does not appear in the *Engishiki*, it is widely believed to refer to Takeminakata & Minomikotohikokamiwake Shrine, the only major shrine in the Minochi District.

Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto is generally considered the offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|qid=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami|1=Yasakatome}}.  

== Genealogy ==
Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|qid=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami|1=Yasakatome}}. His offspring include {{ill|Niwatsume no Kami|ja|庭津女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwatsume no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578874|1=Niwatsume no Kami}}, {{ill|Mase no Kami|ja|馬背神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mase no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578876|1=Mase no Kami}}, {{ill|Yasurao no Mikoto|ja|八須良雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasurao no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578879|1=Yasurao no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Chino no Mikoto|ja|知努命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chino no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578883|1=Chino no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Sanatsurahime no Mikoto|ja|沙南豆良姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanatsurahime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578885|1=Sanatsurahime no Mikoto}} (alternatively identified as Sanatsurao no Mikoto), and {{ill|Takehikoneno Mikoto|ja|武彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehikoneno Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578889|1=Takehikoneno Mikoto}}.  

== Descendant Clans ==
One of the deity's children, Chino no Mikoto, is traditionally believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Chino clan|ja|千野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chino clan|1=Chino clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22130310}}.{{efn|Chino River Myojin, erected November 18, Showa 40, reads: "The deified ancestor of the Chino clan is Chino no Mikoto, a descendant of Takeminakata no Mikoto and Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto."}}  

== Shrines Dedicated to the Deity ==
* {{ill|Takeminakata & Minomikotohikokamiwake Shrine|ja|健御名方冨命彦神別神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata & Minomikotohikokamiwake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Shinshū Shinmachi|ja|信州新町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshū Shinmachi|1=Shinshūshinmachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1042382}}, {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|qid=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}})  
* {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|lt=Tsumashina Shrine|lt=Tsumakusa Shrine|ja|妻科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumakusa Shrine|qq=}} (Nagano City, Minami Nagano)  
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya Wakamisosha|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮 摂社・若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya Wakamisosha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578893|1=Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya Wakamisosha}} ({{ill|Miyagawa|en|Miyagawa|qid=Q1665818|lt=Miyakawa|1=Miyagawa}}, {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|qid=Q838660|lt=Chino, Nagano|1=Chino, Nagano}})  
* Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya Wakamiya Shrine ({{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|qid=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa|1=Shimosuwa}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|en|Suwa District, Nagano|qid=Q1131473|lt=Suwa District|1=Suwa District, Nagano}})  
* Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya Wakamiya Shrine (Suwa District, Shimosuwa)
